summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Benevolence Log UI feature including /benevolence/log page, summary cards, filters, Distributions form benevolence toggle, and sidebar navigation. All 11 backend tests passed. All 8 UI features verified working correctly.
passed_tests: GET /api/benevolence-log response structure contains trust_id, trust_name, distributions, monthly_aggregates, yearly_aggregates, total_all_time, total_count, incomplete_documentation_count
Benevolence log distributions contain required fields (distribution_id, beneficiary_name, amount, date, is_benevolence)
Monthly aggregates use YYYY-MM format with total_amount and count
Yearly aggregates use integer year with total_amount and count
POST /api/distributions with is_benevolence=true creates benevolence distribution
POST /api/distributions validates benevolence_recipient_name is required when is_benevolence=true
POST /api/distributions validates benevolence_need_description is required when is_benevolence=true
critical_code_review_comments: Minor hydration warning in React: <span> inside <tbody> - not affecting functionality but should be fixed for cleaner console output
seed_data_creation: Test distributions created during backend tests were cleaned up
context_for_next_testing_agent: Benevolence Log UI feature fully tested and working. The /benevolence/log page displays summary cards (This Month, This Year, All Time, Documentation status), has Search Recipient and Date Range filters, and shows benevolence distributions in a table with columns: Recipient, Amount, Date, Need/Purpose, Status, Actions. The Distributions form has a benevolence toggle that reveals 3 additional fields (Recipient Name, Need Description, Benevolence Notes) when enabled. Dashboard governance insights correctly route benevolence documentation issues to /benevolence/log. Currently no benevolence distributions exist for the demo trust so the table shows empty state with 'Create Benevolence Distribution' button.
